Headline: EXTENDED: Bus Substitution Between Pulaski and 54th/Cermak Stations
ShortDescription: EXTENDED: Shuttle buses continue to replace Pink Line service between Pulaski and 54th/Cermak due to unforeseen utility issues during planned weekend work.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
How does this affect my trip?
Shuttle buses continue to replace rail service between Pulaski and 54th/Cermak.

Trains will operate between Pulaski and the Loop, every 15 minutes.

Alternate service through the affected area is available on the #21 Cermak bus route during the normal hours of operation (normal bus fares apply).

Allow extra travel time.

Why is service being changed?
We are performing railroad grade crossing maintenance at Cicero Avenue to ensure that trains continue to operate safely along the Pink Line.

Modified train service will remain in effect through Monday, until completion, while we work to restore normal service after unforeseen utility issues encountered over the weekend.

Headline: Subway trains rerouted to elevated tracks
ShortDescription: Red Line subway trains will be rerouted to the elevated tracks between Cermak-Chinatown and Fullerton in both directions.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
How does this affect my trip?
Red Line subway trains will be rerouted to the elevated tracks between Cermak-Chinatown and Fullerton in both directions.

Trains will not stop at subway stations (see map).

Reaching rerouted Red Line trains you can use shuttle buses anywhere between Fullerton and Clark/Lake where the elevated routing is not directly adjacent to the subway. Other options include:

  • From North/Clybourn station: Walk 1/2 mile north to Armitage and 1/4 mile west to Armitage Brown Line station for Red Line trains.
  • From Clark/Division station: Consider using #22 Clark bus south to Clark/Lake for Red Line trains (board on elevated platforms).
  • From Chicago station: Use #66 Chicago bus west to Chicago Brown Line station (at Franklin) for Red Line trains.
  • From Grand station: Walk approx. 1/2 mi west to Merchandise Mart Brown Line station*, for Red Line trains.
  • Downtown: Board/exit trains at any Lake or Wabash Loop Elevated station.
  • Roosevelt station: Board/exit trains on Green/Orange lines elevated platform.

For faster travel times, use the recommended CTA alternate service listed above. However if needed, a free bus shuttle will operate approximately every 7.5 to 15 minutes between Fullerton and Clark/Lake in both directions.

Allow extra travel time.

Why is service being changed?
We are performing maintenance to ensure trains continue to operate safely along the Red Line.

*Entry/exit at Merchandise Mart station is via the Kinzie auxiliary entrance stairways, and requires a farecard (no vending machines), during overnight hours. To make step-free transfers to Red Line trains during this time, use shuttle buses to connect with Clark/Lake or Fullerton.

Headline: #73 Armitage Temporary Reroute at Armitage/Sheffield
ShortDescription: #73 Armitage buses are temp. rerouted in both directions via Armitage, Halsted, Fullerton, Sheffield and Armitage due to construction near Armitage/Sheffield.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
How does this affect my trip?

#73 Armitage buses are temporarily rerouted in both directions via Armitage, Halsted, Fullerton, Sheffield, then resume their normal route on Armitage.

Allow extra travel time.

Why is service being changed?

Buses are rerouted due to construction near Armitage/Sheffield.
